_notes: Baseline for the chorus-test-revA routing benchmark.
Re-pinned by Issue #3474 Phase 0 (2026-06-10, HEAD d45ded4d) when
the fixture migrated from chorus-test-revA_v18.kicad_pcb to
chorus-test-revA_v21_stripped.kicad_pcb.  v18/v19 predate the
chorus repo's 2026-05-11 netlist repair (commit 5b59e20) and the
Phase 0 U1/U2 regulator-connectivity restoration; routing them to
100% would be fake-manufacturable.
nets_total = 51 is the multi-pad signal-net count from the
v21_stripped PCB after skipping the five power/ground nets in the
case config (+3.3V, +3.3VA, +5V, GNDA, GNDD).  See cases.py.
Measurement provenance: PYTHONHASHSEED=0 kct route ... --manufacturer
jlcpcb-tier1 --backend cpp --placement-feedback
--placement-feedback-budget 5 --iterations 50 --auto-fix
--auto-fix-passes 2 --auto-layers --timeout 1200 --seed 42 (the
issue #3474 pinned recipe; see tests/test_chorus_reach_floor_3237.py
CHORUS_V21_* constants for the full record).  Result: 2/51 strict,
30 partial, 19 unrouted, 101 DRC errors on the routed output.
These numbers are intentionally LOW and honest -- the per-net A*
blowup (#3470), budget starvation (#2802), and partial-vs-strict
gap (#3450) phases of #3474 are expected to raise them.  Justify
any decrease in nets_fully_routed or increase in nets_unrouted in
the PR description per docs (update procedure unchanged).
The nets_unrouted=19 absolute floor is the hard regression
assertion: if it grows to 20, CI fails.  The previous v18-era
baseline (26/46 routed, 8 unrouted, issue #2611) is preserved in
git history under version 'issue-2611-initial'.
